tasks.title: SaaS Tier Pricing Implementation
tasks.description: Implement subscription tier system (Basic/Pro/Enterprise) with feature restrictions and payment integration
tasks.details:
The PRD specifies three SaaS tiers but prices are marked as TODO. Implementation requires:

1. Create `SambaTier` enum (BASIC, PRO, ENTERPRISE) in `backend/domain/samba/tenant/model.py`
2. Add tier-related fields to `SambaTenant` model:
   - `tier: SambaTier`
   - `max_products: int` (1000/10000/unlimited)
   - `max_sourcing_sites: int` (3/all/all)
   - `max_markets: int` (2/5/unlimited)
   - `autotune_enabled: bool` (false/true/true)
   - `subscription_start: datetime`
   - `subscription_end: datetime`
3. Create `TierService` with validation methods:
   - `check_product_limit(tenant_id)` - validate product count
   - `check_sourcing_access(tenant_id, site_name)` - validate sourcing site access
   - `check_market_access(tenant_id, market_type)` - validate market access
4. Integrate Stripe for payment processing:
   - Add stripe-python to dependencies
   - Create `PaymentService` with subscription lifecycle methods
5. Add middleware to enforce tier limits on relevant endpoints

Pseudo-code for tier validation:
```python
async def check_tier_limit(tenant_id: str, resource: str) -> bool:
    tenant = await get_tenant(tenant_id)
    limits = TIER_LIMITS[tenant.tier]
    current_count = await count_resources(tenant_id, resource)
    return current_count < limits[resource]
```
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for TierService validation methods
2. Integration tests for Stripe webhook handling
3. E2E test: create tenant with Basic tier, attempt to exceed product limit, verify rejection
4. E2E test: upgrade tier, verify new limits apply immediately

subtasks.title: Enhance SambaTenant Model with Tier Enum and Subscription Fields
subtasks.description: Extend the existing SambaTenant model to include SambaTier enum, subscription dates, and feature flags for the three-tier system (Basic/Pro/Enterprise)
subtasks.details:
1. Create `SambaTier` enum in `backend/domain/samba/tenant/model.py` with values: FREE, BASIC, PRO, ENTERPRISE
2. Add new fields to `SambaTenant` model:
   - `tier: SambaTier` (default FREE, maps to existing `plan` field logic)
   - `subscription_start: Optional[datetime]` (when subscription began)
   - `subscription_end: Optional[datetime]` (when subscription expires)
   - `autotune_enabled: bool` (false for Basic, true for Pro/Enterprise)
3. Create a tier config constant `TIER_LIMITS` defining per-tier limits:
   - Basic: max_products=1000, max_sourcing_sites=3, max_markets=2, autotune=false
   - Pro: max_products=10000, max_sourcing_sites=-1(unlimited), max_markets=5, autotune=true
   - Enterprise: max_products=-1, max_sourcing_sites=-1, max_markets=-1, autotune=true
4. Create Alembic migration to add new columns
5. Maintain backward compatibility with existing `plan` and `limits` fields during transition
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit test: Verify SambaTier enum values
2. Unit test: Verify TIER_LIMITS constant has correct values for each tier
3. Integration test: Create tenant, verify default tier is FREE
4. Migration test: Run alembic upgrade/downgrade, verify no data loss

4. Update `middleware.py` to use TierService instead of inline logic

subtasks.details:

subtasks.title: Apply Tier Restrictions Across All Feature Endpoints
subtasks.description: Integrate tier validation middleware across all relevant API endpoints to enforce feature restrictions based on subscription tier
subtasks.details:
1. Add TierService dependency injection to relevant routers:
   - `collector.py`: Check sourcing site access before collection
   - `sourcing_account.py`: Validate sourcing account count on creation
   - `account.py`: Validate market account count on creation
   - `collector_autotune.py`: Check autotune_enabled before allowing autotune operations
   - `shipment.py`: Check market access before product registration
2. Create reusable FastAPI dependency functions:
   - `verify_sourcing_access(site_name, tenant_id, session)` - dependency for sourcing endpoints
   - `verify_market_access(market_type, tenant_id, session)` - dependency for market endpoints
   - `verify_autotune_access(tenant_id, session)` - dependency for autotune endpoints
3. Update existing `check_product_limit` and `check_market_limit` to use TierService
4. Add tier-aware error messages with upgrade prompts:
   - '소싱처 추가 제한: Basic 플랜은 최대 3개 소싱처만 지원합니다. Pro로 업그레이드하세요.'
   - '오토튠 기능은 Pro 이상 플랜에서 사용 가능합니다.'
5. Create middleware to check subscription expiry on protected routes
6. Add grace period logic (7 days after expiry before downgrade)
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. E2E test: Basic tier tenant attempts to add 4th sourcing site, verify rejection with upgrade message
2. E2E test: Basic tier tenant attempts autotune, verify rejection
3. E2E test: Pro tier tenant can use autotune and all sourcing sites
4. E2E test: Expired subscription blocks protected features after grace period
5. Integration test: Upgrade tier mid-session, verify new limits apply immediately

tasks.title: AutoTune Stability Enhancement
tasks.description: Improve AutoTune reliability with better error handling, rate limiting, and fallback mechanisms
tasks.details:
PRD roadmap specifies 'AutoTune stability enhancement' as short-term priority. Current implementation in `collector_autotune.py` needs:

1. Enhanced circuit breaker pattern:
   ```python
   class CircuitBreaker:
       def __init__(self, failure_threshold=5, recovery_timeout=60):
           self.failures = 0
           self.last_failure = None
           self.state = 'closed'  # closed/open/half-open
       
       async def call(self, func):
           if self.state == 'open':
               if time.time() - self.last_failure > self.recovery_timeout:
                   self.state = 'half-open'
               else:
                   raise CircuitOpenError()
           try:
               result = await func()
               if self.state == 'half-open':
                   self.state = 'closed'
                   self.failures = 0
               return result
           except Exception as e:
               self.failures += 1
               if self.failures >= self.failure_threshold:
                   self.state = 'open'
                   self.last_failure = time.time()
               raise
   ```

2. Per-sourcing-site rate limiting:
   - Implement adaptive backoff per site
   - Track 429/rate limit responses
   - Automatic cooldown periods

3. Improved price change detection:
   - Threshold-based change detection (ignore < 1% changes)
   - Batch price updates to reduce API calls
   - Priority queue for high-value products

4. Better stock synchronization:
   - Immediate propagation for sold-out detection
   - Delayed propagation for restocks (verify stability)
   - Partial stock updates (specific options only)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Load testing: simulate 10k products with rapid price changes
2. Chaos testing: inject network failures, verify circuit breaker activation
3. Integration tests with mock sourcing APIs
4. Monitor error rates in production warroom after deployment

tasks.title: AI Category Mapping Enhancement
tasks.description: Improve AI-powered category mapping accuracy using Claude Sonnet
tasks.details:
PRD specifies 'Claude Sonnet based' category AI mapping. Current implementation needs enhancement.

1. Improve mapping prompt:
   ```python
   CATEGORY_MAPPING_PROMPT = """
   You are an expert e-commerce category classifier.
   
   Source category: {source_category}
   Product name: {product_name}
   Brand: {brand}
   
   Target marketplace: {target_market}
   Available categories:
   {category_tree}
   
   Return the most appropriate category ID. Consider:
   1. Product type (clothing, shoes, accessories, etc.)
   2. Gender (men/women/unisex)
   3. Age group if applicable
   4. Sub-category specificity
   
   Output format: {"category_id": "12345", "confidence": 0.95, "reason": "..."}
   """
   ```

2. Caching layer:
   - Cache mappings by source_category + target_market
   - Expire cache after 30 days
   - Allow manual override with priority

3. Feedback loop:
   - Track successful/rejected mappings
   - Use successful mappings as few-shot examples
   - Periodic model fine-tuning with accumulated data

4. Batch processing:
   - Group similar products for efficient API calls
   - Parallel processing with rate limiting
   - Background job for large batches
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Accuracy benchmark against manually mapped dataset (target: 95%+)
2. A/B test: old mapping vs new mapping with real users
3. Performance test: 1000 products mapping in < 5 minutes
4. Edge case testing: unusual categories, multi-category products

tasks.title: CLIP Image Filtering Optimization
tasks.description: Enhance CLIP ONNX model-based image classification for model/product cut separation
tasks.details:
PRD mentions 'CLIP ONNX model for model cut/product cut auto-classification'. Current implementation needs optimization.

1. Model optimization:
   - Use quantized INT8 ONNX model for faster inference
   - Batch processing for multiple images
   - GPU acceleration if available (onnxruntime-gpu)

2. Classification logic:
   ```python
   class ImageClassifier:
       LABELS = ['model_cut', 'product_only', 'detail_shot', 'lifestyle', 'size_chart']
       
       async def classify(self, image_urls: list[str]) -> list[dict]:
           # Download images in parallel
           images = await asyncio.gather(*[self.download(url) for url in image_urls])
           
           # Preprocess: resize to 224x224, normalize
           tensors = [self.preprocess(img) for img in images]
           
           # Batch inference
           embeddings = self.model.run(None, {'input': np.stack(tensors)})[0]
           
           # Classify each image
           results = []
           for emb in embeddings:
               similarities = [cosine_sim(emb, self.label_embeddings[l]) for l in self.LABELS]
               label = self.LABELS[np.argmax(similarities)]
               confidence = max(similarities)
               results.append({'label': label, 'confidence': confidence})
           
           return results
   ```

3. Smart image ordering:
   - Product-only images first for main image
   - Model cuts for lifestyle appeal
   - Detail shots for quality assurance
   - Size charts last

4. Integration with R2 storage:
   - Tag images with classification metadata
   - Support manual reclassification
   - Bulk reclassification for existing products
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Accuracy benchmark: 90%+ classification accuracy on test dataset
2. Performance test: 100 images in < 10 seconds
3. A/B test: auto-sorted images vs manual selection conversion rate
4. Edge cases: blurry images, mixed content, text overlays

tasks.title: SmartStore TalkTalk Webhook Integration
tasks.description: Implement SmartStore TalkTalk (chat) webhook for real-time customer inquiry handling
tasks.details:
PRD mentions 'SmartStore TalkTalk webhook integration' for CS automation.

1. Webhook endpoint setup:
   ```python
   @router.post('/cs/talktalk/webhook')
   async def talktalk_webhook(
       request: Request,
       session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_write_session_dependency)
   ):
       payload = await request.json()
       signature = request.headers.get('X-Naver-Signature')
       
       if not verify_signature(payload, signature):
           raise HTTPException(401, 'Invalid signature')
       
       event_type = payload['eventType']
       if event_type == 'MESSAGE_RECEIVED':
           await handle_new_message(session, payload)
       elif event_type == 'READ_RECEIPT':
           await handle_read_receipt(session, payload)
       
       return {'status': 'ok'}
   ```

2. Message handling:
   - Parse inquiry type (order status, product question, complaint)
   - Match to order/product using keywords
   - Auto-suggest response template

3. Template response system:
   ```python
   RESPONSE_TEMPLATES = {
       'shipping_inquiry': '안녕하세요! 주문하신 상품은 현재 {status}입니다. 배송조회: {tracking_url}',
       'stock_inquiry': '안녕하세요! 해당 상품은 현재 {stock_status}입니다.',
       'return_guide': '반품 안내: {return_instructions}',
   }
   ```

4. Notification forwarding:
   - Push notification to seller (Firebase Cloud Messaging)
   - Email digest for accumulated inquiries
   - Urgent flag for time-sensitive issues
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Mock webhook testing with sample payloads
2. Integration test with SmartStore sandbox
3. Template matching accuracy test
4. End-to-end: send message → receive webhook → auto-respond

tasks.title: Multi-Market Order Consolidation
tasks.description: Unify orders from all 18 markets into single dashboard with advanced filtering
tasks.details:
PRD specifies 'multi-market order unified view'. Current implementation in `samba_order_router` needs enhancement.

1. Order aggregation service:
   ```python
   class OrderAggregationService:
       async def fetch_all_orders(
           self,
           session: AsyncSession,
           user_id: str,
           filters: OrderFilters
       ) -> list[UnifiedOrder]:
           # Fetch from DB (already synced orders)
           db_orders = await self.repo.get_orders(filters)
           
           # Optionally sync fresh from markets
           if filters.force_sync:
               await self.sync_from_markets(user_id)
               db_orders = await self.repo.get_orders(filters)
           
           return db_orders
   ```

2. Real-time sync from markets:
   - Polling scheduler for order updates (every 5 minutes)
   - Webhook support for markets that offer it
   - Conflict resolution for duplicate orders

3. Advanced filtering UI:
   - Filter by market, status, date range, amount
   - Search by order ID, customer name, product name
   - Saved filter presets

4. Bulk actions:
   - Mass shipping label generation
   - Bulk status update
   - Export to Excel/CSV
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test order sync from multiple markets simultaneously
2. Verify filtering accuracy with various criteria
3. Performance test with 10k+ orders
4. Bulk action reliability testing

subtasks.title: Implement Sourcing Site ROI Analysis API
subtasks.description: Create backend API endpoint for sourcing site ROI analysis including cost per site, conversion rate, and average profit per product source
subtasks.details: Add new methods to SambaAnalyticsService in backend/domain/samba/analytics/service.py: 1) get_sourcing_roi() that calculates cost, revenue, profit, conversion_rate (orders/products ratio), avg_profit_per_product for each source_site. 2) Join samba_order with samba_collected_product on collected_product_id to get source_site data. 3) Add new router endpoint GET /analytics/sourcing-roi in analytics.py. Response schema: [{source_site: str, total_cost: float, total_revenue: float, total_profit: float, order_count: int, product_count: int, conversion_rate: float, avg_profit_per_product: float}]
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with sample orders linked to collected products from different source_sites. Verify calculation accuracy: conversion_rate = order_count/product_count, avg_profit = total_profit/order_count

subtasks.title: Implement Best/Worst Sellers and Dead Stock API
subtasks.description: Create backend API endpoints for product performance analysis including best sellers, worst sellers, and dead stock identification
subtasks.details: Add methods to SambaAnalyticsService: 1) get_product_performance(limit=10, period_days=30) - returns top/bottom products by sales, profit, quantity. 2) get_dead_stock(days_threshold=60) - identifies products with zero orders in threshold period. Query samba_order joined with samba_collected_product. Add endpoints: GET /analytics/best-sellers, GET /analytics/worst-sellers, GET /analytics/dead-stock. Include fields: product_id, product_name, source_site, brand, total_sales, total_profit, order_count, last_order_date, days_since_last_sale
subtasks.testStrategy: Create test data with varying order frequencies. Verify best sellers sorted by sales DESC, worst by sales ASC, dead stock shows only products with no orders in threshold period

subtasks.title: Implement Brand-Based Analysis API
subtasks.description: Create backend API endpoint for brand-level sales and profit analysis aggregation
subtasks.details: Add get_sales_by_brand() method to SambaAnalyticsService that aggregates orders by brand field. Join samba_order with samba_collected_product on collected_product_id to access brand data. Add GET /analytics/brands endpoint. Return [{brand: str, total_sales: float, total_profit: float, order_count: int, product_count: int, avg_margin_rate: float}]. Handle null brands as '미분류 브랜드'. Sort by total_sales DESC. Add optional period filter (start_date, end_date query params)
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with orders linked to products with various brands. Verify brand aggregation accuracy, null brand handling, and period filtering works correctly

subtasks.title: Create Recharts Visualization Components
subtasks.description: Build reusable Recharts chart components for market share pie chart, revenue trend line chart, and profit margin histogram
subtasks.details: Create frontend/src/components/samba/charts/ directory with: 1) MarketSharePieChart.tsx - ResponsiveContainer + PieChart showing channel sales distribution. 2) RevenueTrendChart.tsx - LineChart with dual axis for sales and profit over time. 3) ProfitMarginChart.tsx - BarChart showing profit margin distribution. Use Recharts 3.6.0 already installed. Apply project's dark theme colors (#FF8C00 accent, #1A1A1A background). Each component accepts data prop and optional dimensions. Include tooltips and legends
subtasks.testStrategy: Render each chart with mock data. Verify responsive behavior, correct data display, tooltip functionality, and theme consistency with existing analytics page

subtasks.title: Integrate Charts and New APIs into Analytics Dashboard
subtasks.description: Update analytics page to display new Recharts visualizations and integrate new API endpoints for comprehensive dashboard view
subtasks.details: Modify frontend/src/app/samba/analytics/page.tsx: 1) Add new API calls to fetch sourcing-roi, best-sellers, dead-stock, brands data. 2) Add collapsible sections for each new analysis type. 3) Integrate MarketSharePieChart showing channel distribution from existing marketTable data. 4) Add RevenueTrendChart using existing daily/monthly trend data. 5) Display sourcing ROI table with conversion rates. 6) Add best/worst sellers cards with top 5 each. 7) Add brand analysis table. Use existing card styling and fmtNum formatter for consistency
subtasks.testStrategy: Load analytics page and verify: all charts render without errors, new API data displays correctly, responsive layout works, performance acceptable with production data volumes

tasks.title: Job Queue Monitoring Dashboard
tasks.description: Enhance warroom with real-time job monitoring, alerts, and intervention capabilities
tasks.details:
PRD mentions warroom for 'real-time autotune logs, collection status per market'. Enhancement needed. 1. Job status visualization: ```tsx // frontend/src/app/samba/warroom/JobMonitor.tsx export function JobMonitor() { const { jobs, refresh } = useJobs(); return (
); } ``` 2. Real-time updates: - WebSocket or SSE for live updates - 30-second polling fallback - Visual notification for job state changes 3. Intervention capabilities: - Cancel running jobs - Retry failed jobs - Pause/resume job queue - Emergency stop all 4. Alerting system: - Configurable thresholds (error rate, queue depth) - Email/Slack notifications - Sound alerts for critical issues
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Real-time update latency test (< 2 seconds)
2. Job cancellation reliability
3. Alert triggering accuracy
4. UI responsiveness under high job volume

tasks.title: Multi-tenant Data Isolation Audit
tasks.description: Verify and enhance tenant data isolation for SaaS security
tasks.details:
PRD mentions 'multi-tenancy structure complete'. Need security audit and enhancement.

1. Data isolation verification:
   - Every query must include tenant_id filter
   - No cross-tenant data access possible

2. Audit implementation:
   ```python
   class TenantIsolationMiddleware:
       async def __call__(self, request: Request, call_next):
           # Extract tenant_id from JWT
           tenant_id = get_tenant_from_token(request)
           
           # Store in request state for use in repositories
           request.state.tenant_id = tenant_id
           
           # Log data access
           logger.info(f"Tenant {tenant_id} accessing {request.url.path}")
           
           response = await call_next(request)
           return response
   ```

3. Repository pattern enforcement:
   - All repositories must accept and use tenant_id
   - SQL query review for missing WHERE clauses
   - Database row-level security (PostgreSQL RLS) as additional layer

4. Testing:
   - Automated penetration testing for IDOR vulnerabilities
   - Cross-tenant access attempt logging

5. Compliance documentation:
   - Data flow diagram
   - Security controls documentation
   - Incident response procedures
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Automated IDOR vulnerability scan
2. Manual security review of all queries
3. Attempt cross-tenant access with manipulated tokens
4. RLS policy verification at database level

tasks.title: Olive Young Sourcing Plugin Enhancement
tasks.description: Improve Olive Young crawler stability and data extraction accuracy
tasks.details:
PRD lists Olive Young as sourcing site using crawling method. Existing file: `plugins/sourcing/oliveyoung.py`

1. Anti-blocking measures:
   ```python
   class OliveYoungPlugin(SourcingPlugin):
       site_name = 'oliveyoung'
       concurrency = 2  # Conservative
       request_interval = 3.0  # 3 seconds between requests
       
       async def search(self, keyword: str, **filters) -> list[dict]:
           # Rotate user agents
           headers = {'User-Agent': random.choice(USER_AGENTS)}
           
           # Use residential proxy if available
           proxy = await self.get_proxy()
           
           async with self.session.get(
               f'{BASE_URL}/search',
               params={'query': keyword},
               headers=headers,
               proxy=proxy
           ) as resp:
               html = await resp.text()
               return self.parse_search_results(html)
   ```

2. Data extraction:
   - Product name with full details
   - Regular price and sale price
   - Review count and rating
   - Stock status per option
   - Category breadcrumb

3. Error handling:
   - CAPTCHA detection and alert
   - Rate limit backoff
   - Stale data detection
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Search functionality with various keywords
2. Data accuracy verification against live site
3. Rate limiting compliance test
4. Long-running stability test (24 hours)

tasks.title: Return/Exchange Management Enhancement
tasks.description: Improve returns and exchange workflow with timeline tracking and automated processing
tasks.details:
PRD specifies 'return/exchange/cancel request unified management'. Current implementation in `returns/` needs enhancement.

1. Timeline-based tracking:
   ```python
   class ReturnTimeline:
       events: list[TimelineEvent]
       
       def add_event(self, event_type: str, description: str, metadata: dict = None):
           self.events.append(TimelineEvent(
               timestamp=datetime.now(),
               type=event_type,  # 'requested', 'approved', 'pickup_scheduled', 'received', 'refunded'
               description=description,
               metadata=metadata or {}
           ))
   ```

2. Automated processing:
   - Auto-approve returns within policy window
   - Auto-generate pickup request
   - Auto-process refund when item received

3. Reason categorization:
   - Size/fit issue
   - Product defect
   - Different from description
   - Simple change of mind
   - Statistics by reason

4. Integration with markets:
   - Sync return status to SmartStore/Coupang
   - Auto-update shipping cost claims
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Full return flow E2E test
2. Timeline event ordering verification
3. Auto-approval rule testing
4. Market sync accuracy

tasks.title: AI Sourcing Recommendation System
tasks.description: Implement AI-powered product sourcing recommendations based on market trends
tasks.details:
PRD mentions 'AI sourcing' for recommending best-selling brand×keyword combinations.

1. Data collection:
   ```python
   class TrendCollector:
       async def collect_musinsa_trends(self) -> list[TrendData]:
           # Fetch ranking data from Musinsa
           rankings = await self.musinsa_client.get_rankings()
           return [TrendData(
               brand=r['brand'],
               keyword=r['category'],
               rank=r['rank'],
               sales_volume=r['sales'],
               date=datetime.now()
           ) for r in rankings]
   ```

2. Recommendation engine:
   - Analyze trending brands × categories
   - Consider seller's current inventory
   - Factor in competition level
   - Predict demand using historical data

3. Scoring algorithm:
   ```python
   def calculate_opportunity_score(trend: TrendData, competition: int) -> float:
       demand_score = normalize(trend.sales_volume)
       competition_score = 1 - normalize(competition)
       trend_momentum = calculate_momentum(trend.history)
       return 0.4 * demand_score + 0.3 * competition_score + 0.3 * trend_momentum
   ```

4. UI presentation:
   - Daily/weekly recommendations
   - One-click add to collection queue
   - Historical recommendation accuracy tracking
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Recommendation relevance evaluation
2. A/B test: AI recommendations vs manual selection sales performance
3. System performance with large datasets
4. User feedback collection and iteration

tasks.title: Product Detail Page HTML Generator Enhancement
tasks.description: Improve automatic detail page HTML generation with templates and CDN optimization
tasks.details:
PRD mentions 'detail page HTML auto-generation with top/bottom templates'. Enhancement needed. 1. Template system: ```python class DetailPageGenerator: def generate(self, product: dict, template: str = 'default') -> str: template_obj = self.templates[template] return f""" {template_obj.header}
{''.join(f'' for img in product['images'])}

{product['name']}

{product['description']}

{self.render_size_chart(product)}
{template_obj.footer} """ ``` 2. CDN image optimization: - R2 URL with resize parameters - WebP format for modern browsers - Lazy loading implementation 3. Template management: - CRUD for custom templates - Preview before publish - Template versioning 4. Market-specific formatting: - SmartStore HTML restrictions compliance - Coupang image size requirements - Mobile-optimized layouts
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Generated HTML validation against market requirements
2. Image loading performance test
3. Mobile rendering verification
4. Cross-market compatibility testing

tasks.title: Forbidden Word Filter Enhancement
tasks.description: Improve forbidden word detection and automatic removal/replacement
tasks.details:
PRD mentions 'forbidden word auto-detection and removal'. Current implementation in `forbidden/` needs enhancement.

1. Market-specific word lists:
   ```python
   FORBIDDEN_WORDS = {
       'smartstore': [
           '가품', '짝퉁', '레플리카', '관부가세',
           # Brand restrictions
           '나이키 정품', '아디다스 공식',
       ],
       'coupang': [
           '무료배송', '당일발송',  # Misleading if not true
       ],
       # ... per market
   }
   ```

2. Detection engine:
   - Exact match detection
   - Fuzzy match (similar characters: 가>ㄱㅏ)
   - Context-aware detection (OK in some contexts)

3. Automatic handling:
   - Remove word completely
   - Replace with allowed alternative
   - Flag for manual review

4. Real-time validation:
   - Check before market submission
   - Warning in product edit form
   - Bulk scan for existing products
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Detection accuracy: no false negatives for known forbidden words
2. False positive rate < 1%
3. Performance: 1000 products scanned in < 10 seconds
4. Market submission success rate improvement tracking
